description:
You are helping a data-science team standardise an old customer list.  
    A small INI file already describes exactly how the CSV must be cleaned, but
    no program has been written yet.  
    Your goal is to (1) read the existing INI configuration, (2) transform the
    raw CSV accordingly, and (3) leave behind both the cleaned data file and a
    concise log that an automated grader can check.

    1. Existing material (already present when you start)
       • /home/user/data/raw/customers.csv  
       • /home/user/config/cleaning.ini  

    2. What the INI file means  
       Section [keep] lists the columns – in the order given – that have to be
       preserved.  
       Section [rename] contains key=value pairs mapping original column names
       to their new names.  Any name that is *not* listed here keeps its
       original spelling.  
       Section [drop] contains a single key ‘policy’.  When the policy is the
       literal string “any”, a data row must be removed if *any* of the kept
       columns is empty.

    3. What you must create  
       a. Directory  /home/user/data/processed  
       b. File       /home/user/data/processed/customers_cleaned.csv  
          • MUST be plain UTF-8 text with Unix line-endings (LF only).  
          • First line is the header row and *must* match exactly the kept
            columns *after* renaming, comma-separated, no spaces.  
          • All subsequent lines are the surviving data rows, in the same order
            as the original file, with column values **also comma-separated with
            no trailing separator or extra whitespace**.  
       c. File       /home/user/logs/cleaning.log  
          • Also UTF-8, Unix line-endings.  
          • Exactly four lines, in the order shown below:  
            1) the literal string: CLEANING COMPLETED  
            2) the literal string: Columns kept:   followed by the final header
               row, **comma-separated and with no spaces**  
            3) the literal string: Rows dropped:   followed by the integer
               count of discarded rows  
            4) the literal string: Rows written:   followed by the integer
               count of rows that landed in customers_cleaned.csv  
          • No additional lines, punctuation, or timestamps.  

    4. Verification contract  
       The automated test will:
       • Confirm that customers_cleaned.csv exists at the exact path above.  
       • Confirm that its SHA-256 digest matches the reference answer.  
       • Inspect cleaning.log, making sure it contains exactly the four lines
         described, with the correct numbers.  

    5. Constraints & hints  
       • Work only with tools available to an ordinary (non-root) user.  
       • Feel free to script in Python, awk, or anything installed in a normal
         minimal Linux distribution.  
       • Be careful about quoting commas and about preserving the *order* of
         columns exactly as requested.  

    When everything is finished you should be able to:

       $ cat /home/user/data/processed/customers_cleaned.csv
       id,name,email_address,age,country
       1,John Doe,jdoe@example.com,29,USA

       $ cat /home/user/logs/cleaning.log
       CLEANING COMPLETED
       Columns kept: id,name,email_address,age,country
       Rows dropped: 4
       Rows written: 1

    (The numbers above are the correct final counts for this dataset.)
truth:
Initial files that exist before the agent starts
    -----------------------------------------------
    1) /home/user/data/raw/customers.csv
       Content (LF line-endings):
       customer_id,full_name,email,age,country,phone
       1,John Doe,jdoe@example.com,29,USA,555-1234
       2,Jane Smith,,34,Canada,555-2345
       3,,third@example.com,27,USA,
       4,Bob Lee,blee@example.com,,USA,555-4567
       5,Alice Wong,awong@example.com,31,,555-5678

    2) /home/user/config/cleaning.ini
       Content:
       [keep]
       list = customer_id,full_name,email,age,country

       [rename]
       customer_id = id
       full_name   = name
       email       = email_address

       [drop]
       policy = any


    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
    -------------------------------------------
    1) /home/user/data/processed/customers_cleaned.csv (chmod 644)
       Content (LF line-endings, **exactly**):
       id,name,email_address,age,country
       1,John Doe,jdoe@example.com,29,USA

    2) /home/user/logs/cleaning.log (chmod 644)
       Content (four lines, LF line-endings, **exactly**):
       CLEANING COMPLETED
       Columns kept: id,name,email_address,age,country
       Rows dropped: 4
       Rows written: 1
